fields.value: Previous insurance chatbots required escalation to a human agent

The AI Claim Assistant guides customers through first notice of loss for auto property damage claims using natural conversation, eliminating wait times and human involvement for routine filings. Between 85–90% of customers now complete their claim submission entirely through the AI system.
claim professionals redirect their effort toward complex cases requiring human judgment
Travelers processed more than 1.5 million claims and paid over $23 billion in losses last year, with catastrophe events alone capable of generating 100,000+ claims within days.

The system does not replace claim adjusters; instead, it handles the high-volume, standardized intake phase, freeing human experts to focus on coverage disputes, liability determination, and other decisions requiring contextual knowledge or judgment calls.

value: Between 85–90% of customers now complete their claim submission entirely through the AI system
eliminating wait times and human involvement for routine filings
24/7 support that does not create bottlenecks during surge events
improving customer satisfaction during high-stress moments

evidence.quote: Travelers Insurance deployed a fully autonomous voice system powered by OpenAI’s Realtime API across all US states as of June 2, 2026, according to the OpenAI Blog.
Where previous insurance chatbots required escalation to a human agent, Travelers’ autonomous system completes 85–90% of initial claims intake without any handoff

The AI Claim Assistant guides customers through first notice of loss for auto property damage claims using natural conversation, eliminating wait times and human involvement for routine filings. Between 85–90% of customers now complete their claim submission entirely through the AI system after the nationwide rollout
Travelers Insurance deployed a fully autonomous voice system powered by OpenAI’s Realtime API across all US states as of June 2, 2026
claim professionals redirect their effort toward complex cases requiring human judgment
Travelers processed more than 1.5 million claims and paid over $23 billion in losses last year, with catastrophe events alone capable of generating 100,000+ claims within days.
By connecting OpenAI’s frontier models to its internal claims infrastructure and orchestration systems, Travelers enables 24/7 support that does not create bottlenecks during surge events

The Realtime API’s capacity to maintain natural dialogue while integrating with backend systems, retrieving policy details, and submitting structured claims data differentiated it from competing approaches.

The system does not replace claim adjusters; instead, it handles the high-volume, standardized intake phase, freeing human experts to focus on coverage disputes, liability determination, and other decisions requiring contextual knowledge or judgment calls.
Between 85–90% of customers now complete their claim submission entirely through the AI system after the nationwide rollout, which occurred within two months of launching in eight pilot states.

evidence.quote: Baseten deployed a comprehensive inference platform for Poolside that combines dedicated model hosting with the Baseten Inference Stack, the Baseten Frontier Gateway, billing infrastructure and training data tooling, all operational within weeks of the partnership kicking off.

As Poolside prepared to launch its Laguna model family to the global AI community in April 2026, the team faced a fundamental infrastructure challenge: how could they serve a frontier-scale model to thousands of developers with a branded production-grade API but without having to build the operational infrastructure themselves?

Baseten deployed a preview version of Poolside’s Laguna M.1 model as a whitelabeled Model API accessible via an OpenAI-compatible interface directly through Baseten production infrastructure. Within 48 hours of Poolside creating their account, the model was live and responding to queries.
